market.advice: Pre-open setup looks healthy into the US open: SPY at 744.78 sits comfortably above its 50-day (735.91), breadth is a solid 68% of stocks above their 50-day MA, VIX is calm and falling (15.8, 5-day avg 16.5), and only 1 distribution day is on the tape with a follow-through day intact -- no sign of institutional distribution. QQQ also holds above its 50-day. Leadership keeps broadening away from megacap tech -- NVDA, MSFT, TSLA, ORCL, PLTR all screen WATCH/WEAK here, several well off their highs -- into regional/money-center banks ahead of Q2 earnings season, REITs, healthcare, utilities (AI-power-demand angle) and select industrials. Geopolitical headlines (Khamenei funeral proceedings in Tehran, the NATO Ankara summit, Iran-related oil-export stories) are keeping energy-news flow elevated but haven't dented breadth or spiked the VIX. Into the open: normal position sizing, lean into the rotation (banks/utilities/REITs/healthcare) rather than chasing extended megacap tech, and be selective on names already >15% below their 50-day -- that's a repair, not a breakout.

picks.name: The Bank of New York Mellon
picks.thesis: The cleanest setup in the whole scan: a 2-contraction VCP tightened to 3.0% with the RS line at new highs, EPS +42%/revenue +13% YoY, and a forward P/E of just 14x. No TradingView rating resolved for BK, so this call rests entirely on the scan's own data.
strengths: Tightest VCP in the financials group (2 contractions, 3.0%) with RS at new highs
EPS +42% / revenue +13% YoY funds the setup
Only 2.5% off the 52-week high, cheap at 14x forward earnings
risks: Earnings in 12 days (Jul 15) -- binary event risk into the print
No independent TV read available -- flying on the scan's data alone
watch: Trim into strength ahead of the July 15 print
$147.29 52-week high is the breakout trigger

picks.name: Eli Lilly and Company
picks.thesis: GLP-1 leadership continues to compound: EPS +170%/revenue +56% YoY off the obesity and diabetes franchise, a 5-contraction VCP tightened to 3.1%, and RS at new highs. TradingView's STRONG_BUY (15/1/10) fully confirms.
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY, near-unanimous (15 buy/1 sell)
EPS +170%/revenue +56% YoY -- best fundamental momentum on this list
RS line at new highs -- leading, not lagging, the market
risks: Priced at 27x forward earnings -- no room for a stumble
Earnings Aug 5 -- still a month out, less of a near-term catalyst
watch: New 52-week highs confirm continuation
RSI 60 -- healthy, room to run before overbought

picks.name: American Electric Power
picks.thesis: The AI-power-demand utility trade in its cleanest form: a 3-contraction VCP tightened to 2.6% right at the 52-week high, and TradingView's STRONG_BUY (16/0/10) with zero sell votes fully agrees.
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY, zero sell votes
Sitting essentially at the 52-week high with a tight 2.6% base
Defensive sector with a genuine secular growth driver (data-center load growth)
risks: RSI 73 -- stretched short-term, a pullback to the 50-day would be healthy before adding
Fwd P/E 20x is rich for a regulated utility
watch: Breakout above the 52-week high on volume confirms; a rejection here would flag exhaustion

picks.name: Duke Energy
picks.thesis: Second utility name riding the same power-demand rotation as AEP -- tightest VCP of the group at 2.0% -- with TradingView's STRONG_BUY (17/0/9) the strongest of any name on this list.
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY, 17 buy/0 sell -- the strongest TV consensus in this scan
Tightest VCP in utilities (2.0%) -- coiled and ready
EPS +12%/revenue +11% YoY, solid for a regulated utility
risks: RSI 67 and only 2.1% off the 52-week high -- limited room before resistance
Rate-sensitive; a hawkish surprise from the Fed would pressure the whole group
watch: Group-wide rotation into utilities/REITs is the tailwind -- watch for that rotation to stall

picks.name: DTE Energy
picks.thesis: Third leg of the utility rotation -- essentially at its 52-week high with TV's STRONG_BUY (16/0/10) confirming, though the negative EPS growth print (Michigan rate-case timing) is a wrinkle the market is looking through.
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY, zero sell votes
Revenue +16% YoY -- the underlying business is growing even as reported EPS lags on rate-case timing
risks: Headline EPS growth is negative (-44%) -- read the footnotes on regulatory timing before sizing up
RSI 73, stretched short-term
watch: A new 52-week high confirms; watch for the group rotation (AEP/DUK/DTE) to broaden further into utilities

picks.thesis: Industrial/logistics REIT with EPS +40% YoY and TradingView's STRONG_BUY (17/0/9) -- zero sell votes -- fully agreeing with our score.
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY, zero sell votes
EPS +40%/revenue +13% YoY funds continued distribution growth
risks: Small-cap REIT -- thinner liquidity than the mega-cap names on this list
Fwd P/E 38.7x is rich; priced for continued execution

picks.thesis: Best-in-class mall REIT posting EPS +16%/revenue +19% YoY with RS at new highs -- premium retail traffic remains resilient. TV's STRONG_BUY (16/0/10) backs the call.

Revenue +19% YoY outpaces most REIT peers on this list
risks: Earnings Aug 11 -- furthest out on this list
Fwd P/E 33.5x prices in continued strength

picks.thesis: Grocery-anchored shopping-center REIT rounding out the real-estate sleeve -- EPS +29% YoY with TV's BUY (15/2/9) confirming, at a much more approachable price point than SPG/PSA.

Cheapest entry point of the REIT names on this list

Tightest VCP in industrials (2.1%)

picks.name: GE Aerospace
picks.thesis: Aerospace engine cycle remains the strongest industrial story out there -- RS at new highs and revenue +25% YoY -- but RSI at 91 is about as extended as a chart gets, so this is a name to watch for an entry, not chase here.
strengths: TV BUY (14/3/9) confirms
RS at new highs, revenue +25% YoY on the aftermarket/engine cycle
risks: RSI 91 -- extremely overbought, highest reading of any pick on this list

picks.name: Howmet Aerospace
picks.thesis: Same aerospace-cycle tailwind as GE without the extended RSI -- EPS +71% YoY and TV's BUY (14/3/9) confirms, with a healthier RSI of 56.

RSI 56 -- room to run unlike GE
risks: Fwd P/E 44.9x is rich
Earnings Aug 6, still a ways out

picks.thesis: Energy-drink category leader with RS at new highs, EPS +29%/revenue +27% YoY, and TV's BUY (15/3/8) confirming -- a defensive-sector name still growing like a discretionary one.
strengths: RS at new highs, TV BUY confirms

Fwd P/E 37.7x prices in continued category growth

EPS +33% YoY -- the turnaround plan is showing up in the numbers

strengths: EPS +34%/revenue +11% YoY -- fab-tool demand remains strong
TV BUY leans positive
risks: 16.6% below the 52-week high -- still in repair mode, not a fresh breakout
Earnings Aug 14, still six weeks out

picks.thesis: Best fundamentals of any chip name here (EPS +58%/revenue +35% YoY) but TradingView's NEUTRAL (8/8/10) is a genuine split verdict, not a confirmation -- worth flagging since our score says STRONG BUY-quality but the independent read is a coin flip.
strengths: EPS +58%/revenue +35% YoY -- the strongest fundamentals in the semiconductor group
Cheapest fwd P/E of the mega-cap chip names at 21x
risks: TV NEUTRAL (8 buy/8 sell/10 neutral) directly disagrees with our STRONG BUY-caliber score -- treat as a WATCH, not a green light
9.1% off the 52-week high, still basing
Earnings Jul 16 -- 13 days out
watch: Wait for TV's read to tip toward BUY, or a fresh 52-week high, before treating this as confirmed

picks.name: Corning Incorporated
picks.thesis: Turnaround story with the best EPS growth on this entire list (+139% YoY, fiber/optical-connectivity demand from AI datacenter buildouts), but the chart is still 23% off its high and TV's NEUTRAL (8/8/10) says the jury's still out.
strengths: EPS +139% YoY -- the single best fundamental print in this whole pick list
AI datacenter fiber-connectivity demand is a genuine secular tailwind
risks: TV NEUTRAL directly contradicts the STRONG BUY-caliber score -- a real disagreement worth sizing around
23% below the 52-week high -- this is a name still finding its footing, not confirmed

picks.name: Airbnb, Inc.
picks.thesis: Solid fundamentals (revenue +18% YoY) and RSI 73 near the highs, but no TradingView rating resolved yet to cross-check against -- flying without the second opinion on this one.
strengths: Revenue +18% YoY, at the 52-week high
5-contraction VCP shows a maturing base

RSI 73 -- already stretched, less room to run before a pullback
